day_contexts: Day 1 — First strikes, Minab school (165+ killed), Iranian retaliation
Day 2 — Beit Shemesh (9 killed), Port Shuaiba (6 US KIA), sustained bombing
Day 3 — Hezbollah enters; Strait closure; friendly fire incident
Day 4 — Cluster munitions confirmed; embassy/media strikes
Day 5 — Iranian navy destruction begins; Kuwait girl killed
Day 6 — 500+ ballistic missiles fired; Bapco refinery hit
Day 7 — One week; reduced tempo; tugboat sunk (3 crew missing)
Day 8 — Oil infrastructure strikes begin; Brent approaches $100
Day 9 — Cluster munition kills 2 workers in Yehud; Bapco force majeure
Day 10 — New Supreme Leader; 16 minelayers destroyed; maritime traffic down 97%
Day 11 — 5+ vessels hit in 24 hrs; IEA 400M bbl release
Day 12 — French soldier killed; KC-135 crash (6 US KIA); 30+ minelayers destroyed
Day 13 — Zarzir strike (~60 wounded); 2 workers killed in Oman; IDF ground war in Lebanon
Day 14 — Kharg Island struck; Fujairah fire; AUMF debate; 15,000+ targets
Day 15 — Continued sporadic fire; 7 ballistic salvos at Israel
Day 16 — Conflict enters third week; cumulative cost ~$20B
Day 17 — Larijani + Soleimani killed; 2 Israeli civilians Ramat Gan; Dubai airport struck; Brent $106
Day 18 — Brent pulls back to $101; S&P recovers; Ford fire contained; war cost ~$21.7B
Day 19 — South Pars struck; Khatib killed; IRGC threatens Gulf energy; Brent $108; 3,200 vessels stranded
Day 20 — Iran retaliates: Ras Laffan, SAMREF, Kuwait hit; Hezbollah 100+ rockets; Brent $119 intraday; Fed holds; carrier gap
Day 21 — 7,800+ targets; 4 senior officials killed; F-35 struck; 300 attack waves on Israel; Haifa BAZAN refinery hit; Jerusalem Old City; Boxer deploys
Day 22 — Nowruz/Eid; 8,000+ targets; 130 vessels destroyed; Natanz struck; Diego Garcia attacked; Dimona hit; 9 salvos at Israel; 47 hospitalized; 12 medics killed in Lebanon
Day 23 — Trump 48-hr ultimatum; Cooper: Iran declining; IDF wave in Lebanon; Dimona 180-200 injured; Tripoli arriving; war cost ~$27.3B

Day 24 — Trump postpones power plant strikes 5 days; IDF strikes Tehran; Iran strikes Netanya; 3 Litani bridges destroyed; HRW: war crimes in Lebanon; Urmia/Khorramabad residential areas struck; gas $3.96; oil crashes then recovers

Day 25 — 7 Iranian missile waves on Israel; 4 Tel Aviv impacts (6 wounded); IRGC attacks 5th Fleet Bahrain (1 Emirati KIA); IAF 50+ targets; Dimona sirens; Nesher cluster munition; Israel DM: occupy Lebanon to Litani

Day 26 — IDF wide-scale attacks on Tehran govt infrastructure; south Tehran 12 killed/28 wounded; 82nd Airborne deploying; Habbaniyah strike kills 7 Iraqi soldiers; 6 Peshmerga killed near Erbil; missile hits Tel Aviv street; Kuwait airport drone strike; 33 Lebanese killed in 24 hrs

Day 27 — Israel kills IRGC Navy Cmdr Tangsiri + senior staff in Bandar Abbas; 'wave of extensive strikes' on Isfahan; Iranian missiles hit Kafr Qasim, Ganei Tikva, Kiryat Ata, Tel Aviv; 2 killed in Abu Dhabi from debris; Bahrain fire; Hezbollah rocket kills man in Nahariya; IDF soldier killed in Lebanon; Sgt. Pennington KIA announced; total 13 US KIA, 290 WIA

Day 28 — IDF overnight blitz in Tehran and western Iran; 6 civilians killed in Qom; casualties in Urmia from overnight strikes; Hezbollah 100+ rockets at Israel; woman killed Nahariya; IDF Sgt. Volansky killed S. Lebanon; 10 wounded by missiles in Israel; 2 killed Abu Dhabi from debris; Kuwait shoots down 2 drones

Day 29 — Houthis launch ballistic missiles at Israel (Beersheba/Dimona area); Iranian missiles strike 8 sites in Tel Aviv (1 killed, 2 injured); 10 US troops injured at Prince Sultan AB Saudi Arabia; Kuwait airport radar damaged by drone; Hezbollah escalates to 600 rockets/missiles/drones in 24 hrs; Iran missile launch rate declining — stock depletion

Day 30 — Islamabad quad summit; Israel strikes Arak heavy water + Ardakan yellowcake; IDF ~170 Hezbollah sites over weekend; 800+ Hezbollah killed per IDF; 2 Israeli soldiers seriously wounded + 7 moderately; 5 Hezbollah rockets at Galilee; 3 journalists killed in Jezzine; 9 paramedics killed Mar 28; air raid sirens Bahrain/Kuwait; IRGC ultimatum on universities expires noon

Day 31 — Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran infrastructure; Pentagon plans 'weeks of ground operations'; Bandar Khamir struck (5+ killed); UAE intercepts 16 ballistic missiles + 42 drones; Kuwait detects 14 missiles + 12 drones; Saudi intercepts 10 drones; Iran strikes chemical plant Ne'ot Hovav + Haifa refinery + Kuwait water plant; HRANA Day 30: 17 civilians + 3 military killed; 14 US KIA confirmed; 'Janfada' volunteer recruitment; Iraq PMF enters Iran; 3rd 'No Kings Day' protests

Day 32 — Iranian drone strikes Kuwaiti VLCC Al Salmi at Port of Dubai; Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure threatened — dual chokepoint; 2 Chinese container ships transit Hormuz (first since war); Bushehr NPP 3 projectiles near perimeter — IAEA warns radiological risk; IDF reaches Litani tributary; 1,200+ killed in Lebanon since Mar 1 (124+ children); 13 US KIA / 290 WIA (255 RTD); Iran deputy health minister: 1,937 killed / 24,800+ wounded; gas crosses $4.00; gold $4,578-4,600; Islamabad talks stalled

Day 33 — IRGC cluster bomb hits Bnei Brak: girl (10-12) critically wounded, boy (13) seriously hurt; 3 missile waves at Israel; tanker Aqua 1 struck off Qatar; Kuwait airport fuel depots ablaze; Bahrain fires; Beirut Jnah strike: 7 killed, 26 wounded; Hezbollah cmdr Hashem killed; Houthi missile at Israel intercepted; 200 dynamic strikes; former US Embassy Tehran struck; Tofigh Daru factory (10 nurses killed); Fujairah: Bangladeshi worker killed by drone shrapnel; 15 US KIA / 348 WIA; Iran threatens 17 US corporate HQs

Day 34 — S&P 6,582.69 (+0.11%) — dramatic reversal on Iran-Oman transit protocol; WTI $111.54 first close above $100; 4 IRGC cluster-warhead salvos; Hezbollah 50+ rockets; IDF 170 targets; Kharazi injured/wife killed; Hashem killed; 15 KIA / 348 WIA; 35-nation Hormuz coalition; 28% approval

Day 35 — Good Friday — markets closed; Ford departs Split resumes Eastern Med ops; Bush transiting Atlantic; 3 CSGs committed; Houthis resuming Israel attacks but holding fire on shipping — Bab al-Mandeb closure 'likely' if escalation; Lebanon: 1,318+ killed, 3,935+ wounded, 1.2M displaced; IAEA severed; Zarif Foreign Affairs proposal; ceasefire talks stalled; Apr 6 deadline 3 days

Day 36 — F-15E Strike Eagle shot down (first US combat aircraft loss since 2003); A-10 downed during rescue; Pakistan ceasefire talks collapse; Houthis 4 attacks in 7 days; CMA CGM first major Western Hormuz transit; French/Japanese ships follow; 1,725+ ballistic missiles and 4,446+ drones launched by Iran; Lebanon: 1,345+ killed; IDF: disarming Hezbollah 'unrealistic'; Iran rejects 48-hr ceasefire; Apr 6 deadline 2 days

Day 37 — WSO rescued after 48+ hours inside Iran; Mahshahr petrochemical zone struck (5 killed, 170+ injured); Iran BMs at IDF HQ Tel Aviv + Neot Hovav; IDF Sgt. Guy Ludar killed in Lebanon (friendly fire); Hezbollah rockets 60km into Israel (Umm al-Fahm); 5th Houthi missile intercepted; HRANA: 3,519+ killed (1,607 civilians, 244+ children); Hengaw: 7,300+; Health Min: 2,076+; 15 US KIA / 365 WIA

Day 38 — Trump sets Apr 7 8 PM ET deadline; 45-day ceasefire rejected by Iran; IAEA zero access to enrichment facilities — 440.9 kg at 60% (enough for ~10 weapons); Bushehr struck 4 times total (Apr 4 last — 1 protection staff killed, 75m from perimeter); Rosatom evacuated 198 staff; 1,200+ Lebanese killed, 3,500+ wounded; 3 UNIFIL Indonesian peacekeepers killed; Hezbollah 100s of rockets daily; IDF permanent buffer zone south of Litani; 15 US KIA / 140-520+ WIA

Day 39 — US strikes 50+ targets on Kharg Island; Israel wide-scale bridge/railway strikes (Qom bridge, Kashan railway — 2 killed/3 injured, Tabriz-Zanjan freeway, Karaj railway); 18 civilians killed in Alborz (incl. 2 children); 6 children killed in Tehran overnight (4 girls, 2 boys under 10); Tehran synagogue destroyed; IRGC Maj. Gen. Khademi + Quds Force Bakeri killed; Asaluyeh petrochemical complex struck; Haifa residential building hit (4 killed); cluster munitions in Tel Aviv (~20 impacts); Iran missile at Israel Apr 7; coordinated Houthi-Iran-Hezbollah barrage; Lebanon: 1,497 killed, 4,639 wounded; 15 US KIA / 520+ WIA

Day 40 — ETERNAL DARKNESS: Israel launches single largest coordinated strike of war on Lebanon — 50 fighter jets, 160 munitions, 100+ targets in 10 min across central Beirut, southern Beirut, Bekaa Valley, Sidon, Tyre — 254 KILLED, 1,160+ wounded (deadliest single day); Lebanon declares Apr 9 national day of mourning; Netanyahu: ceasefire does NOT include Lebanon — 'we still have goals'; Iran re-closes Strait of Hormuz accusing Israel of violating ceasefire; UN Human Rights Chief: 'nothing short of horrific'; European leaders + EU issue joint statement demanding Lebanon's inclusion; CENTCOM confirms cumulative: 13,000+ targets, 80% air defenses, 150 warships, every Iran sub sunk, 97% mines; Iran: 2,076+ (Health Min) / 3,636 (HRANA Apr 7) / 7,300+ (Hengaw)

Day 41 — CEASEFIRE FRACTURING: Hezbollah launches retaliatory missiles on northern Israel early Apr 9 — 'grave violation of ceasefire' (Hezbollah lawmaker Fadlallah); IDF admits overestimated 2024 damage — Hezbollah still has hundreds of launchers, tens of thousands of rockets; Soufan Center: ceasefire 'hovers on the verge of collapse'; no confirmed US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~45 hours); 1,530-1,754+ Lebanese killed cumulative; 9,100+ Iranians killed; 15 US KIA / 520+ WIA; White House confirms Vance + Witkoff + Kushner for Islamabad talks Apr 10-11; Pope Leo XIV Apr 11 peace vigil; UNHCR: 3.2M Iran IDPs, 1M+ Lebanon displaced (700K in shelters)

Day 42 — CEASEFIRE ~72 HRS — VANCE TO ISLAMABAD: No strikes on Iranian territory ~72 hours; Israel 50+ strikes in Lebanon (Ali Yusuf Harshi killed — Qassem's nephew); Trump asked Netanyahu 'more low-key'; 1,888+ Lebanese killed, 6,092 injured; UNICEF: 600 children killed/injured; WHO warns hospitals may exhaust supplies; Hezbollah rockets at Kiryat Shmona/Metula/Misgav Am — no Israeli casualties; Iran: 2,076 (Health Min) / 3,636 (HRANA) / 4,700+ (Iran Intl) / 5,300+ (Hengaw); 13 US KIA / 373+ WIA (CENTCOM); former FM Kharazi dies; VP Vance departs Islamabad; Bush CSG arriving; Pope vigil tomorrow; March CPI released

Day 43 — ISLAMABAD TALKS BEGIN: First direct US-Iran face-to-face since 1979 — VP Vance meets Ghalibaf/Araghchi at Serena Hotel; ceasefire holds ~96 hours on Iran front (zero Iranian deaths since Apr 7); Israel strikes 120 Hezbollah sites in 24 hrs — 14 killed Apr 10 incl. 13 Lebanese State Security in Nabatieh; Hezbollah fires 30+ rockets at northern Israel (Kibbutz Misgav Am, Deir al-Asad school, Safed, Kiryat Shmona); 1 IDF NCO severely wounded by suicide drone; Lebanon cumulative: 1,888-1,953 killed, 6,303+ wounded; Iran frozen assets dispute — Tehran claims US agreed, US flatly denies; Ghalibaf carries blood-stained bags to talks; Pope Leo XIV peace vigil at St. Peter's noon ET; March CPI 3.3% YoY (gas +21.2% MoM largest since 1967); UMich sentiment 47.6 all-time low; 13 US KIA / 357-381 WIA; no new Iranian casualties; HRANA total: 3,636 (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military)

Day 44 — ISLAMABAD TALKS COLLAPSE — TRUMP DECLARES HORMUZ BLOCKADE: Talks end pre-dawn with no agreement after 21 hours; ceasefire on Iran front technically holds ~120 hours (zero new Iranian casualties); Netanyahu: Israel struck 200+ Hezbollah targets over Apr 11-12 weekend; Lebanon cumulative: 2,020+ killed, 6,436+ wounded, 1.2M displaced (200K+ fled to Syria, 472 schools converted to shelters); 'Black Wednesday' Apr 8 toll now reported 254-357 killed, 1,150+ wounded (Civil Defense 254, AP 303, Wikipedia 357); Pope Leo XIV Apr 12: 'closeness to the people of Lebanon' — 'moral obligation to protect them'; Iranian delegation arrived Islamabad dressed in black, carrying shoes and bloodied belongings of Minab schoolchildren; frozen assets dispute derails talks; Araghchi-Witkoff reportedly nearly came to blows over Hormuz; Ghalibaf five-point X post blames US; Trump Truth Social: US Navy will BLOCKADE Hormuz, interdict every vessel paying Iran's toll, 'BLOWN TO HELL' any Iranian firing at US; 13 US KIA / 381 WIA (CENTCOM) unchanged; Iran casualties: Health Min 2,362 (WHO Apr 10), HRANA 3,540-3,636, Hengaw 6,620 military, Iran Intl 4,700+ security, CSM 'at least 3,000'; WHO: 48 hospitals damaged, 25 health workers killed in Iran; IFRC $18M medical supplies blocked; Lebanon-Israel talks Apr 15 at State Dept (first contact since 1983); PM Salam postpones Washington trip

Day 45 — BLOCKADE EFFECTIVE 14:00 GMT — IRGC WARNS OF 'HARSH AND DECISIVE' FORCE: Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~144 hours (zero new Iranian casualties since Apr 7); Trump's US naval blockade takes effect; CENTCOM narrows scope to Iranian ports only; IRGC declares military vessels approaching Strait trigger ceasefire violation; Lebanon cumulative (Public Health EOC via CGTN Apr 12): 2,055 killed, 6,588 wounded — up from 2,020/6,436 Day 44; Apr 12: Hezbollah ~20 rockets + drones (Kfar Yuval, Yir'on, Kiryat Shmona 1:20 AM barrage); IDF raids Bint Jbeil Governmental Hospital killing ~20 Hezbollah; Netanyahu visits southern Lebanon with Katz, Zamir — declares 'war continues'; Katz announces Operation 'Silver Plough' to demolish border homes; Israeli forces ram UNIFIL vehicles with Merkava tank on two occasions; Apr 13: Sirens across Galilee for drone attacks including near Acre; IDF reports close to capturing Bint Jbeil — 100+ Hezbollah operatives killed in battle; Lebanese Red Cross paramedic killed by Israeli drone (NBC News); 13 US KIA / 381 WIA (CENTCOM) unchanged; Intercept disputes: 15+ KIA, 520+ total injuries (DCAS internal 23% above CENTCOM public); Iran casualty gap nearly 4x: Health Min ~2,000 / HRANA 3,636 / Hengaw 7,650 (1,030 civilians, 6,620 military) / IDF 6,000+ IRGC; 55,500 Afghan refugees returned to Afghanistan; WHO Dubai hub ON HOLD; UNHCR $80M appeal for 2.8M affected in Iran; 3.2M Iran IDPs (compounded by flooding in 24 provinces); 9 Litani bridges destroyed in Lebanon; WHO: 77 healthcare attacks in Lebanon, 53 health worker deaths; food prices +17-20% MoM in Lebanon; Iran executed at least 14 protesters Mar 17-Apr 6 (Hengaw); Lebanon-Israel talks Apr 15 at State Dept

Day 46 — BLOCKADE FIRST FULL DAY — LEBANON LOSSES MOUNT AS IRAN FRONT FROZEN: Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~168 hours (zero new Iranian casualties since Apr 7); no new strikes on Iranian territory Apr 13-14, no Iranian retaliatory strikes — rhetoric escalated but no kinetic action; Lebanon cumulative Apr 14: 2,089+ killed (+34 from Apr 13, +69 from Apr 11 baseline of 2,020), 6,762+ wounded (+174 from Apr 13, +326 from Apr 11); 252 women + 166 children + 88 medical workers killed since March 2; UNICEF: 600+ children killed or injured in Lebanon since Mar 2; Apr 8 'Black Wednesday' single-day toll documented at 357 killed (50 jets dropped ~160 bombs on 100 targets in 10 minutes); IDF forced evacuation orders cover ~14% of Lebanon's territory (1,470 sq km); 1,000,000+ displaced (~20% of Lebanon's population); 200,000+ fled to Syria; Battle of Bint Jbeil continues (Day 5-6): IDF 98th Division within 300-400m of the stadium — site of Nasrallah's 2000 'spider's web' speech — 100+ Hezbollah fighters killed, dozens remain active; IDF raided hospital where armed Hezbollah operatives were sheltering; 2 IDF soldiers moderately wounded + 6 lightly wounded by Hezbollah drone attack in southern Lebanon Apr 13; woman injured Nahariya Apr 13 by Hezbollah rocket; ICRC center struck in Tyre, one killed; Iran casualty figures unchanged — gap nearly 4x spread across sources: Health Min ~2,000+ (understated per IHRNGO), HRANA 3,636 (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military, 254+ children, 240+ women), Hengaw 5,300+ military alone (Mar 18 baseline), IDF 6,000+ IRGC killed — combined extrapolated ~9,100+; ACLED 3,000+ distinct conflict events across 29 of 31 provinces; 13 US KIA / 381 WIA unchanged (CENTCOM); Intercept disputes: 15+ KIA, 520+ total injuries, DCAS 23% above CENTCOM; Israel: 26 killed, 7,693 wounded, 10 IDF KIA in Lebanon; 28+ killed Gulf states (Bahrain, Kuwait, UAE); 118+ killed Iraq (mostly PMF); 1 French soldier (Erbil), 1 Moroccan national (Bahrain); Iran 3.2M IDPs compounded by flooding in 24 provinces; WHO Dubai hub ON HOLD — 50+ emergency requests delayed; UNHCR $80M appeal for 2.8M affected in Iran; Hezbollah SecGen Qassem rejects Apr 15 Israel-Lebanon talks as 'pointless' and 'a free concession'

Day 47 — IRAN COUNTER-BLOCKADE THREAT / CEASEFIRE ~192 HRS / LEBANON +43 IN 24 HRS: Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~192 hours (zero new Iranian casualties since Apr 7); no kinetic action by Iran despite IRGC Maj. Gen. Ali Abdollahi's counter-blockade threat on state TV encompassing Persian Gulf + Sea of Oman + Red Sea; CNN Apr 14: satellite imagery shows Iran RECONSTITUTING missile capabilities during ceasefire — equipment, dump trucks, front-end loaders visible at blocked tunnel entrances near underground missile bases at Khomeyn and south of Tabriz; US intel: ~50% Iran missile launchers survived first 40 days; CNN Apr 11: China preparing air defense deliveries to Iran within weeks; Lebanon Public Health EOC Apr 14: 2,089 killed, 6,762 wounded — TASS later update per Health Ministry: 2,124 killed (Δ +35 within same day); +43 Lebanese killed in past 24 hours per manifest; 168 children, 254 women, 87-91 health workers killed; UNICEF: 600+ children killed or injured in Lebanon since Mar 2; Hezbollah launches ~30 rockets at northern Israel Apr 15 morning (IDF confirmed); Hezbollah claimed 24 separate attacks Apr 14 on northern Israel + IDF positions in S. Lebanon; shot down Israeli Hermes 450 drone and fired SAMs at Israeli warplanes; Battle of Bint Jbeil near-complete per IDF: 'several dozen' Hezbollah remain, 100+ killed since Apr 9 launch; Apr 14: 10 IDF paratroopers wounded (3 severely) in clash; 3 Hezbollah killed; 1 IDF soldier killed + 13 total wounded in separate incidents; armored battalion commander seriously wounded; 3 Radwan Force fighters captured + turned over to Unit 504 interrogation; IDF Northern Command Chief Maj. Gen. Milo visits for assessment; Apr 15: IDF 8th Brigade (91st Division) uncovered anti-tank missile launcher + weapons caches; struck Hezbollah command center; 5 IDF divisions operating (36th, 91st, 98th, 146th, 162nd); 'Operation Silver Plough' demolishing all border village structures; Haaretz: IDF operating 'using methods similar to those used in Gaza'; since March 2 Hezbollah killed ≥12 IDF soldiers + 2 civilians; IDF Home Front Command warned Hezbollah may temporarily increase attacks to influence Washington negotiations; Lebanon-Israel direct talks Washington Apr 14 (first since 1983) — Leiter + Moawad + Rubio — 2+ hours, Israel refused ceasefire commitment pressed Hezbollah disarmament, Lebanon demanded ceasefire + displaced return, both agreed to further talks; Hezbollah Qassem rejected talks as 'futile' 'free concession'; 500+ Iraqi PMF attacks since late February (FPRI); US diplomatic team ambushed by PMF in Baghdad post-ceasefire; Houthis in 'strategic pause' on Red Sea shipping since Oct 2025 Gaza ceasefire but joined Iran war with BMs on Israel Mar 28; US Operation Rough Rider struck 1,000+ targets in Yemen since Mar 15; Iran's Apr 15 Red Sea threat may signal potential Houthi activation; Iran cumulative: Health Min 2,076+, HRANA 3,636 (1,701 civ / 1,221 mil / 714 unclassified), Iran Intl 4,700+ security, Hengaw 6,620 military (Apr 8), Iran Red Crescent ~2,000 fatalities/27,000 injuries (Apr 10); 212+ children killed per Iran MoH; 13 US KIA / 381 WIA unchanged (CENTCOM); Intercept disputes: 15+ KIA, 520+ injuries (Ford fire + F-15 shoot-down + A-10 crash); 1 non-combat death (Maj. Sorffly Davius Kuwait) excluded from CENTCOM; 118+ Iraqis killed (mostly PMF); regional displacement 4M+: Iran 3.2M (UNHCR Mar 12, likely higher), Lebanon 1-1.2M internal (IOM), 300K+ Lebanon→Syria (OCHA Apr 7), 140K+ in 680 shelters (OCHA Apr 14); Iran infrastructure (OCHA Apr 3): 115,193 civilian units damaged, 763 schools, 334 health/emergency centers, 23 WHO-verified healthcare attacks → 9 deaths, 120+ heritage sites; Lebanon infrastructure (OCHA/WHO Apr 13-14): 55 primary healthcare centers closed, 6 hospitals closed, 133 WHO-verified healthcare attacks → 80+ killed / 190+ injured, 18 incidents affecting water infrastructure, 40,000+ homes destroyed; Lebanon Flash Appeal $308M requested, <1/3 funded

Day 48 — CEASEFIRE ~216 HRS ON IRAN FRONT / LEBANON TOLL 2,167/7,061 / NABATIEH STRIKE KILLS 19 INCL 13 STATE SECURITY / IDF CHIEF ZAMIR: S. LEBANON A 'KILL ZONE': Ceasefire on Iran front holds ~216 hours (zero new Iranian casualties since Apr 7); Hegseth (Pentagon Apr 16): US 'locked and loaded' to restart combat if talks collapse; WSJ reports Trump weighing 'resumption of limited military strikes' — NOT implemented; Iran cumulative (reporting frozen Apr 7): Health Min 2,076+, HRANA 3,636 (1,701 civ / 1,221 mil / 714 unclassified), Iran Intl 4,700+ security, Hengaw 6,620 military (Apr 8), Red Crescent ~2,000 fatalities/27,000 injuries incl. 4,000 women + 1,621 children (Apr 10); combined extrapolated ~9,100+; ACLED (Apr 15 17:00 CEST): ≥70 fatalities abroad from 560+ outbound Iranian attacks across 14 countries, 619+ interceptions, 163 energy-sector events; MSNBC regional aggregate 'nearly 6,000'; Trump Truth Social Apr 14-15 claim of 42,000 protesters killed flagged as UNSUPPORTED (HRANA ~7,000, Iran govt 3,100+); IDF's 6,000 IRGC KIA claim remains unverified; Lebanon MoPH end-Apr 15 (via WAFA/Al Jazeera): 2,167 killed / 7,061 wounded, ≥166 children, 250+ women, 51-53 health workers — up from ~2,089 Apr 13 baseline (+78); Apr 15 events: early AM Hezbollah ~20 rockets into Galilee; Lebanese civil defense recovered four bodies in Qadmus; five killed in IDF strike on Ansariyah; last functioning Tebnin hospital struck; Apr 15 Hezbollah rocket wounded 5 IDF soldiers (1 serious, 4 light); Apr 16 IDF strike on NABATIEH killed 19 people including 13 Lebanese State Security personnel; Hezbollah fired missile at Ashdod (claimed Ashdod Naval Base); missile damaged 1,500-yr-old church in Nahariya; IDF 98th Division consolidated Bint Jbeil — 100+ Hezbollah fighters killed cumulatively since Apr 9, 3 surrendered Apr 15; IDF Chief Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir called south Lebanon a 'kill zone'; 5 IDF divisions operating (36th, 91st, 98th, 146th, 162nd); 'Operation Silver Plough' demolishing border village structures; Alma Center: cumulative Hezbollah rocket/UAV since Mar 2 = 1,121 attack waves; Israeli security cabinet convened evening Apr 15 to debate proposed 1-week Lebanon ceasefire — ENDED WITHOUT DECISION (Channel 12); Trump floated 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire Apr 16 (NBC) — UNCONFIRMED by either party; Hezbollah MP Hussein Hajj Hassan condemned Apr 14 Washington talks as 'a grave sin'; Rubio-Aoun phone call Apr 16 on Lebanon ceasefire; First direct Israeli-Lebanese ambassador talks in decades took place Apr 14 in Washington mediated by Rubio; 19 UN Human Rights experts Apr 15 called Israel's Apr 8 Lebanon strikes 'blatant violation of UN Charter' flagging potential crimes against humanity; Lebanon displacement: >1.2M (IOM); 140,682 in 684 collective shelters (OCHA Apr 12); >200,000 Lebanese/Syrians crossed into Syria Mar 2-27; displacement orders cover ~1,470 km² (~14% of Lebanon); ICRC delivered first medical shipment into Iran Apr 12 — first since war start; WHO Lebanon: 60+ tonnes medical supplies mobilized since Mar 18 via EU Humanitarian Airbridge; Lebanon Flash Appeal $308.3M, 30.6% funded; WHO Flash Appeal $30M for 5 most-affected countries incl. $10M for Lebanon; UNICEF reached 188,155 IDPs in 360+ shelters (Apr 1); WHO EMRO Director Balkhy Apr 14: '1 in every 7' people in the 750M-person EMR region now need humanitarian assistance; no confirmed major outbreak; insulin stockout reported in Lebanese primary care; respiratory/eye risks from refinery strikes; Iran: up to 3.2M IDPs (UNHCR Mar 12 — figure likely stale); 4.4M Afghan refugees at elevated risk; 70,000+ returns to Afghanistan by mid-March; partial power blackouts confirmed Tehran/Karaj; WHO logged 13+ health sites struck by Mar 5; Pasteur Institute, Khatam-al-Anbia and Gandhi hospitals damaged; Bushehr NPP auxiliary building hit Apr 4; Mahshahr Petrochemical Zone struck Apr 4 (5 killed, 170 injured); 81,000+ civilian units damaged per Iran Red Crescent (61K homes, 19K commercial, 481 schools, 30+ universities, 120+ heritage sites); all Iranian ports blockaded since Apr 13; WHO Lebanon: 92-133 attacks on healthcare, 51-53 health workers killed / 91-137 injured; Qaaqaiyet el Jisr and Delaffi bridges plus Litani crossings destroyed — isolating the south; 472 schools repurposed as shelters; Sultaniye power station damaged; water pipelines in Marjaayoun hit; 13 US KIA + 1 non-hostile / 381 WIA unchanged (CENTCOM); Intercept: 15+ KIA, 520+ injuries; Israel cumulative ~26 total (19+ civ, ~6-10 IDF; wires cite 40 incl. 27 civ / 13 mil as of Apr 7); 7,451-7,693 wounded (≥418 mil); Iraq 118 (incl. 85 PMF KIA + 6 US airmen KC-135); Yemen (Houthis) ~6 (ACLED); Gulf states aggregate ~28 killed, hundreds wounded; Iran MoH figure FROZEN SINCE ~APR 7 — any Iranian death-toll field carries 'reporting suspended' badge

Day 49 — LEBANON CEASEFIRE IN FORCE / IRAN CEASEFIRE ~240 HRS / LEBANON TOLL 2,167-2,196 / BINT JBEIL CONSOLIDATED / BUSHEHR NO NEW STRIKES: Lebanon 10-day ceasefire took effect 5:00 PM ET Apr 16 — second diplomatic front opens alongside still-holding Iran ceasefire; Trump declared US-Iran deal 'very close' Apr 17; Pakistan Army Chief Munir + Naqvi carried Washington's terms into Tehran Apr 15-16, Iran agreed 'in principle' to third-party nuclear monitoring (4 countries + IAEA); enrichment-moratorium gap: US seeks 20 yrs, Iran offers ~5, 10-yr compromise on the table; Iran cumulative (reporting frozen Apr 7): Health Min 2,076 / HRANA 3,636 / 3,300+ per forensics chief (NBC/Al Jazeera); 26,500+ injured; 115,000+ buildings damaged; Lebanon MoPH/NNA: 2,167-2,196 killed (incl. 172 children, 93 health workers), 7,061 wounded, 1.2M displaced; US 13 KIA (combat) + 2 non-combat, 303 WIA (CENTCOM/Pentagon); Israel 26-40 killed (26 per Al Jazeera tracker), 7,453-7,693 wounded; Gulf states 32 (UAE 13, Kuwait 10, Saudi 2, Oman 3, Bahrain 1) + 400+ injured from 31 nationalities; Iraq 118 (mostly PMF) + 1 French soldier, 139 PMF injured; Lebanon saw war's most intense 24 hrs before truce — IDF struck 380+ Hezbollah targets incl. airstrike on Nabatieh and pre-dawn Hezbollah rocket barrage on Kiryat Shmona (fires, no casualties); Battle of Bint Jbeil: Brig. Gen. Guy Levy's 98th Division executed most consequential operation to date — Egoz Unit commandos raided Hezbollah 'combat compound' in Aynata casbah, seized RPGs, Kalashnikovs, grenades, an anti-aircraft missile, surveillance gear, then demolished ~70 Hezbollah infrastructure sites in under a minute via coordinated detonation; IDF recovered 130+ weapons stashed in a Bint Jbeil school alongside Hezbollah flags; Levy delivered 'Bint Jbeil 2026: Our forces control the area' address from captured stadium — same venue as Nasrallah's 2000 'spider's web' speech; Netanyahu declared Israel 'going to eliminate this large Hezbollah stronghold'; cumulative Bint Jbeil toll: 100+ Hezbollah fighters killed; Hezbollah counter-claimed 39 operations including a D9 bulldozer hit by suicide drone; ceasefire MoU 6-point: extension conditional on Lebanon 'effectively demonstrating its ability to assert its sovereignty'; Israel retains self-defense rights; IDF troops remain south of Litani; IDF spokesman Adraee warned displaced not to return south yet; Hezbollah not a signatory, declared 'finger on the trigger'; DM Katz: Lebanon campaign 'not yet completed'; tens of thousands of Lebanese IDPs began moving southward Apr 17 despite warning; Iran deputy FM Apr 17 rejected any 'temporary' ceasefire extension; Hegseth Apr 17: US forces 'locked and loaded' to resume strikes on Iran's power grid if talks fail before Apr 22; Senior US military official told HASC Apr 16 Iran-backed Shiite militias have conducted 'hundreds' of attacks on US forces since Feb 28, Iran retains 'thousands of missiles' and one-way attack drones; Wilson Center: Houthi tempo declining — 1 ASBM intercepted Apr 11, ASBM into Gulf of Aden Apr 13, 4 drones destroyed Apr 14, no confirmed major Red Sea shipping strikes Apr 16-17; House rejected H.Con.Res.38 WPR 213-214 Apr 16 (Massie lone GOP yes, Golden lone Dem no, Davidson present); Senate rejected WPR 47-52 Apr 15 (Rand Paul lone GOP yes, Fetterman lone Dem no); 60-day WPR deadline ~May 1; infrastructure: 309 Iranian health facilities + 42 ambulances damaged, 7 Iranian hospitals evacuated, 700+ educational facilities damaged, 120+ historical sites; Lebanon: 133 WHO-verified healthcare attacks, 80+ health-worker deaths, 55 primary care centers + 6 hospitals closed, 14 hospitals damaged; OCHA Lebanon flash appeal $308.3M through May; UNHCR 202,477+ cross-border flows from Lebanon into Syria since Mar 2 (incl. 27,343 Lebanese nationals); UNICEF RRM in Lebanon reached 188,155 IDPs across 360+ shelters; Bushehr NPP: no new strikes Apr 16-17 — Apr 4 projectile strike was 4th incident, killed one physical-protection staffer, no radiation increase;
